reviews.review_description: The controls don't move your character enough to the side, not very intuitive. One handed mode made it playable. You can jump from easy level tracks to the hard mode tracks and the camera will follow you down, allowing you to complete hard mode tracks. Easy and hard level difficulties should be separated more clearly, preventing this.

District M is a sweet new rhythm game that is has some cool beats to groove to. You get to control a rollerblade character while keeping sync with the music!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KmrP0jrfAPw

You can see it included as in my Top 10 App Lab games video series here: https://youtu.be/0YXaguvpL9Y

Unique game with fun motion mechanics, well worth a go.
Unlike other 'relaxing' & 'meditate' games that somehow feels a bit forceful, this one actually gives off a relaxing and easy feel vibe to it. Need a casual fun chill game to relax? this is the one.

developer_response.body: Thank you :) Super happy you vibed with the game <3

reviews.review_description:
Something I just keep coming back to time and time again, the one handed control method made this a much more enjoyable experience. Very excited for the full release 

Most original game on the quest, there's elements of olli olli 2 and subway surfers here but there's nothing else like it on quest.
This is going to be a beautiful game to escape into. Wonder if they'll add in tricks?

the demo shows the main mechanics of the game. easy to pick up and control. swing your arms to move your skater and jumo around also. great music and level design. the free roam camera givea you a different perspective when playing alao which is a nice feature. look forward to seeing more levels  check out game play in the following video.

District M Demo - Rhythm VR game and Third Person Platformer
https://youtu.be/5bdPwTmHpWE

developer_response.body: SLIDE to the left, SLIDE to the right Criss-Cross... lol. Loved your video! And ooof you are so good at it already! Can't wait to throw more challenging levels at you.

reviews.review_description: This is a neat idea for a rhythm game. I see a lot of potential for it. Very cool from the third person perspective.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your support! Tighten up, tis gonna get crazy :)

reviews.review_description:
Very original game, and good dynamics of game play. I think the game has a bright future - imagine multiplayer!  They did a GREAT job with the tutorial too, with live videos embedded in game play showing someone controlling it so you can mimic their movements.  It's easily the best tutorial mode I've seen on a Quest game. 

Can't wait for more.  Now shut up and  take my money!!  :)
I think the music tracks in this game are great, and the levels that accompany them make it even better! I definitely recommend you try out this game for yourself.

This game took me back to roller skating in the 70’s and 80’s in such a cool way. The sights and sounds are fantastic making it just fun to play. I can’t wait to see it in its entirety and I’m sure you will too. Get this game!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing! We tried other motions like running, flying but roller skating gave the best feeeels. See you at the playtests :)

reviews.review_title: very cool 3rd person view and music
reviews.review_description: very cool 3rd person view, the funky music and colorful environments give you really happy vibes !

developer_response.body: You are the best vibes :) Thank you :)

reviews.review_title: Bright colors, fun music, fresh concept!
reviews.review_description: District M places you in a bright colorful world where the concept is skating and rhythm-based mechanics. The demo conveys the imagery and main concepts well, but can fall short after playing the single available level. This is not the reason I took a star off my review. The camera allows you the freedom to watch from any angle, which can become a problem. At one point, I was in front of my avatar, wondering where the character was. Then, trying to get the camera back to an ideal position took enough time that it was better to just start the level over. The next track is unavailable, but looks amazing, so I am definitely looking forward to the full release.

I like it!!! The song and the style of the scene are kind of Indie Pop, refreshing (vs. current sport-rhythm game)
the tutorial is very good, can see how the hand work (vs. other games)
Suggestion, 
(1) the tracking needs to be more in-time, precise (controller <=> movement)
(2) 1st person's view would be more exciting, durable for playing (repetition)
(※ or tutorial maintain 3rd person view, but playing in first person?!)
(3) need more spice, e.g. dog rush out of the road, piano falling ...etc.

developer_response.body:
Yay!! Yesss we too can't wait for you to play more levels!!! 

1) Agreed that we can make controls a bit more precise but trust me, it is already very close. So it is like a sport, you get better at it with more practice.

2) We really really want to make a 3rd person for VR. I can assure you that the intensity of the upcoming levels will make you forget what perspective it is.

3) DOG RUSH YESSSS!!! This demo is a small peek at our game. We have a long way to go! Please keep such sharing ideas with us.

reviews.review_description:
BGM is top notch,Graphics are funky & modern. Yes it is too short for now but hope developers will add more levels as it is just the beginning.
Gameplay if you wanna see how it looks https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q3hNt_hz9Cw

A unique VR rhythm game in a vibrant and wacky world. You control, from a distance, a toy roller blade character through windy roads and a narrowly avoid obstacles by swaying your arms to the beat of the music. The demo offers one level to get familiar with the controls and a free fly option that lets you change your camera position to create interesting and cinematic clips. Also adds a new challenge to the game. The sway mechanic takes some getting used to with correct speed and timing but certainly lots of potential in this title!

developer_response.body: Wacky world lol. Exactly! Thank you for playing, King! You will forever be a district m OG haha :) As we put out more levels for you, I am sure the game's mechanic will feel a lot more natural. Show us your Free Roam videos!!

reviews.review_description: I really like the game and the concept. The controls need to be tuned a bit. Sometimes my hands are in the center and the character is moving to the right. I also think a first person feature would be absolutely stunning in a game like this. Last when I played the level that is unlocked after the tutorial my character didn’t move, however my camera did so I was just floating along the world. This may have been me doing something wrong but I don’t know.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing, King! Yeah, there was a bug that caused that 'moving to the right' action... the devs got to it :) The second level you played is just a teaser of what's coming next in the game and isn't playable. Sorry for the confusion oops. That level will be part of our Early Access launch!
